_note: Two-plane decomposition for ADR-0086 ui-radio-group[variant=segmented] — the both-orientations + shared-moving-indicator redesign (Kim, 2026-07-06). OUTSIDE-IN nodes = the variant's surfaces on the existing group; INSIDE-OUT actions = what the variant must DO. Derived independently, then crossed via hosts. plan:true — each leaf carries the checkable acceptance predicate the builder's tests must satisfy (no source exists yet; this is the design manifest the ADR + the later build slice read).

nodes.label: ui-radio-group[variant=segmented] (the variant on the existing group)
group public API (props surface)
variant prop — reflected 'default'|'segmented' on groupProps
nodes.accept: `npm test radio-group` — jsdom: `variant` reflects; default `'default'`; `[variant='segmented']` present on the host after set; `attributes[]` in radio-group.md gains the row

nodes.label: orientation prop — reflected 'horizontal'|'vertical' on groupProps (newly minted; none exists today)
nodes.accept: `npm test radio-group` — jsdom: `orientation` reflects; default `'vertical'`; after `variant=segmented` with NO explicit orientation the host reflects `orientation='horizontal'` at connect (variant-derived default, resolved once)

nodes.label: group layout surface (the segmented track)
grid track model — display:grid, equal 1fr cells, orientation-driven main axis
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser radio-group` — a rendered segmented group is `display:grid` with equal cells (segment inline-sizes equal ±1px horizontal; block-sizes equal vertical); no inter-cell gap; the group is a real bounding box (not collapsed)

nodes.label: outer rounded track + collapsed inter-segment dividers
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser` — group outer `border-radius == --ui-radius-base`, `border:1px solid --md-sys-color-neutral-outline`; adjacent segments share ONE divider line (no double border); the first segment (`:first-of-type`) suppresses its leading divider

nodes.label: shared moving indicator (one artifact, animated)
indicator geometry — group ::before, one-cell size, position:absolute, z-index behind segment text
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser` — the group `::before` is one-cell-sized (`inline-size ≈ trackWidth/count` horizontal; `block-size ≈ trackHeight/count` vertical), `position:absolute`, stacked BELOW the segment text; the group carries NO `overflow:hidden`

nodes.label: indicator translate positioning — transform off --index/--count (NOT grid-track placement)
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser` — the `::before` `transform` translate == `selectedIndex × cellSize` (translateX horizontal / translateY vertical); `grid-column`/`grid-row` is NOT used to place the indicator (proves the animatable path is transform)

nodes.label: indicator motion — transition transform over --ui-motion-fast + reduced-motion gate
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser` — `::before` `transition-property` includes `transform` over `--ui-motion-fast`/`--ui-ease-standard`; selecting a different segment changes the transform toward the new cell; under `prefers-reduced-motion:reduce` the `::before` `transition` computes `none` (jump)

nodes.label: indicator fill + selected/unselected ink (accent, non-color signifier)
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser` — the backed (selected) cell resolves fill `--md-sys-color-primary-selected` + ink `--md-sys-color-primary-on-primary`; unselected ink `--md-sys-color-neutral-on-surface-variant`; EXACTLY one cell is backed (fill-presence); a checked segment does not wash on hover (selection holds); with no selection the indicator is hidden (opacity 0)

nodes.label: segment presentation (each ui-radio → a button cell)
dot suppression + label centering
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser` — the radio `::before` dot computes `display:none` in segmented mode; the segment centers its label (`display:inline-flex; justify-content:center; align-items:center`)

nodes.label: segment geometry — the Pattern-class control-height ramp
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser` — segment `blockSize == getComputedStyle(--ui-height-md)`; `padding-inline == blockSize/2` (±ε, slotless-edge pad); `padding-block == 0`; line-height read via the `--ui-control-line-height` custom property (== '1') OR computed `line-height == font-size × 1` px — NEVER asserted `=== '1'`

nodes.label: segment interaction states — hover/active on unselected, disabled-idle, selected-holds
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser` — unselected hover → `--md-sys-color-primary-container-low`, active → `--md-sys-color-primary-container`; a group under `effectiveDisabled` shows NO hover/active lift (idle holds)

nodes.label: state seam — --ui-radio-group-index / --count written from .ts on the commit path
nodes.accept: `npm test radio-group` — after a commit the host style carries numeric `--ui-radio-group-index` (== selected index) and `--ui-radio-group-count` (== segment count), refreshed on every commit + seeded at connect (the --value-pct state-custom-prop precedent, range-element.ts:111)

nodes.label: roving orientation resolution — pass the resolved orientation to the existing rovingFocus call
nodes.accept: `npm test radio-group` (jsdom + browser) — horizontal: ArrowRight/Left move focus+selection; vertical: ArrowDown/Up; Home/End jump to ends; the roving axis matches the resolved orientation (no dead-key mismatch)

nodes.label: forced-colors treatment — indicator Highlight, selected ink HighlightText, frame/ink ButtonText
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser` — under emulated forced-colors: indicator → `Highlight`, selected ink → `HighlightText`, unselected ink + frame/dividers → `ButtonText`; the indicator STILL positions (transform applies under forced-colors); the focus outline survives

nodes.label: focus-ring reuse — the radio's existing :focus-visible outline, unclipped
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser` — a focused segment shows the `:focus-visible` outline (not `none`), NOT clipped by the indicator or the group (no overflow:hidden); forced-colors-safe (`--md-sys-color-focus-ring → Highlight`, ADR-0009)

nodes.label: descriptor + per-orientation keyboard doc (radio-group.md)
nodes.accept: `npm test` — `attributes[]` in radio-group.md mirrors `static props` (ADR-0081 trip-wire green); the keyboard table lists the axis PER orientation (no false 'ArrowLeft/Right under vertical roving' claim)

nodes.label: reused single-select machinery (unchanged — no commit path touched)
nodes.accept: `npm test radio-group` — the existing radio-group suite stays green: single-select exclusivity, roving tabindex, group form value, required→valueMissing validity all unchanged

actions.label: select the variant presentation
choose the orientation (horizontal or vertical stack)
lay the segments out as equal cells regardless of label length
draw the joined track with shared dividers
render ONE shared fill behind the selected segment
position the shared fill over the selected cell
animate the fill sliding between selections
respect reduced-motion — no slide, jump
suppress the dot glyph + center the label
size each segment to the control-height ramp
give unselected segments hover/active feedback
suppress hover/active feedback when the group is disabled
hold the selected fill (a checked segment cannot toggle off / wash)
signal the selection without relying on color (fill presence)
invert distinctly under forced-colors
preserve the focus ring (survives, not clipped)
rove with the arrow axis matching the orientation
preserve single-select / value / validity (unchanged)
expose the selection index + segment count to CSS
keep the descriptor + keyboard doc truthful

edges.why: the grid/segment CSS selects on [variant=segmented] — the prop must exist before the layout keys off it
the grid main axis (column vs row) selects on the resolved [orientation]
the roving trait reads the resolved orientation to pick the Left/Right vs Up/Down axis
the translate math (100%/count, index×100%) assumes the gapless equal-1fr cells the grid establishes
the indicator must be an absolutely-positioned one-cell ::before before it can be translated
the transform reads --ui-radio-group-index / --count, which the .ts state seam writes
the transition targets the transform positioning; motion has nothing to animate until the translate path exists
vertical: the indicator's per-cell block-size resolves to the control-height ramp
the forced-colors block inverts the base accent fill + inks to Highlight/HighlightText
the descriptor's attributes[] row mirrors the variant prop
the per-orientation keyboard table mirrors the roving axis the resolution sets
